title: Managed audit manual sandbox connection credential resolver runtime shell chain stop-or-prerequisite decision record

sourceNodeV303.sourceSpan: Node v301 + Node v302 + Java v136 + mini-kv v133

decisionRecord.sourceSpan: Node v303 + Java v136 + mini-kv v133
decisionRecord.decisionReason: Node v303 aligned the post-decision intake echoes, but the runtime shell chain still lacks explicit operator approval, credential-handle readiness, no-network safety tests, abort semantics, and upstream echo of the prerequisite decision.

requiredPrerequisites.label: Operator approval artifact
requiredPrerequisites.currentEvidence: missing: no signed operator approval artifact has been produced for runtime shell implementation

requiredPrerequisites.label: Credential handle readiness
requiredPrerequisites.currentEvidence: missing: only credential handle/review status can be referenced; credential value reading remains forbidden

requiredPrerequisites.label: Raw endpoint allowlist review
requiredPrerequisites.currentEvidence: missing: endpoint handle can be reviewed, but raw endpoint URL parsing/rendering remains forbidden

requiredPrerequisites.label: No-network safety tests
requiredPrerequisites.currentEvidence: missing: no test has proven runtime shell code cannot dial managed audit before explicit approval

requiredPrerequisites.label: Manual abort and rollback semantics
requiredPrerequisites.currentEvidence: missing: abort semantics are documented as required, but no executable runtime shell abort contract exists

requiredPrerequisites.label: Java/mini-kv prerequisite echo
requiredPrerequisites.currentEvidence: missing: Java v141 and mini-kv v134 have not yet echoed this stop/prerequisite decision

explicitNoGoConditions.condition: Any next step asks Node to implement runtime shell code.
Any next step asks Node to invoke a runtime shell.
Any next step asks Node, Java, or mini-kv to read credential values.
Any next step asks Node to parse or render a raw endpoint URL.
Any next step asks Node to instantiate providers or resolver clients.
Any next step asks Node to send HTTP/TCP to managed audit.
Any next step asks Java or Node to write ledger/schema state.
Any next step asks mini-kv to run LOAD/COMPACT/RESTORE/SETNXEX or become authority.

necessityProof.blockerResolved: v303 aligned the post-decision plan intake echoes, but did not decide whether the runtime shell chain should stop or require explicit approval prerequisites.
necessityProof.consumer: Java v141 and mini-kv v134, then Node v305
necessityProof.whyV303CannotBeReused: v303 verifies upstream echo alignment only; it does not enumerate missing prerequisites or publish a decision that Java and mini-kv can echo before any later runtime-shell discussion.
necessityProof.existingReportReuseDecision: Reuse v303 as source evidence, but create v304 as the minimal decision layer that records stop/prerequisite requirements.
necessityProof.stopCondition: Stop immediately if the next step requires credential values, raw endpoint URLs, provider/client instantiation, HTTP/TCP, runtime shell implementation or invocation, ledger/schema writes, mini-kv write/admin commands, or automatic upstream start.

warnings.message: v304 documents missing prerequisites and recommends upstream echo only; it does not approve runtime shell implementation.

recommendations.message: If the chain continues, run Java v141 and mini-kv v134 in parallel as read-only echoes of this decision.
Keep runtime shell implementation blocked until explicit approval artifacts and no-network safety evidence exist.

evidenceEndpoints.recommendedParallelJavaV141: Java v141 runtime shell chain stop/prerequisite decision echo
evidenceEndpoints.recommendedParallelMiniKvV134: mini-kv v134 runtime shell chain stop/prerequisite non-participation receipt

nextActions: Archive Node v304 as a blocked stop-or-prerequisite decision record, not as runtime shell approval.
If continuing the chain, ask Java v141 and mini-kv v134 to echo this decision in parallel before Node v305.
Keep credential values, raw endpoint URLs, provider clients, external requests, managed audit connections, schema migration, ledger writes, LOAD/COMPACT/RESTORE/SETNXEX, and auto-start blocked.
